# Protocol Conformance Test - Strict MCP Protocol Compliance
# Demonstrates: protocol validation, field requirements, format checking

# Test required jsonrpc field
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"test"}
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'

# Verify ID is present in response
stdout '"id":1'

# Verify ID is not null
! stdout '"id":null'

# Test either result or error (mutual exclusivity)
# Valid responses have either result OR error, never both
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}
stdout '"result":'
! stdout '"error":'

# Or error without result
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"invalid"}
stdout '"error":'
! stdout '"error":.*"result":'

# Notification format (no ID required or allowed)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/cancelled","params":{"requestId":1}}
! stdout '"id":'

# Batch request format
setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":101,"method":"echo"}]
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":101'

# Array response format
stdout '\['
! stdout 'null.*null'

# Test string and integer IDs are both valid
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"string-id","method":"test"}
stdout '"id":"string-id"'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":42,"method":"test"}
stdout '"id":42'
